pi_items.verified_value: Not standard Forest Green - standard is #228B22 (RGB 34,139,34); task uses #2E7D32 (RGB 46,125,50)
pi_items.source: Web search: Forest Green standard hex codes from Figma, HTML Color Codes, and CSS specifications confirm #228B22 is the standard. Task's #2E7D32 is a different green shade.
Task description and verifier.py line 33 both specify this hex code for ellipses

pi_items.verified_value: Not standard Amber - standard is #FFBF00 (RGB 255,191,0); task uses #FF8F00 (RGB 255,143,0)
pi_items.source: Web search: Amber standard hex code is #FFBF00 per Figma, HTML Color Codes, and multiple design resources. Task's #FF8F00 is orange-leaning amber, not standard.
Task description and verifier.py line 34 both specify this hex code for titles

pi_items.verified_value: Not standard Dark Blue - standard is #00008B (RGB 0,0,139); task uses #0D47A1 (RGB 13,71,161)
pi_items.source: Web search: Dark Blue standard hex code is #00008B per Figma, HTML Color Codes, and CSS specifications. Task's #0D47A1 is a royal/medium blue, not standard dark blue.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic 4-slide LibreOffice Impress presentation (.odp format) with 2 rectangles and 1 ellipse per slide, all initially colored gray (#C0C0C0) with black title text (#000000). The task requires recoloring rectangles to #2E7D32, ellipses to #FF8F00, and titles to #0D47A1. All structural metadata about slides and shape counts is verified correct. However, the color names are NOT standard web/CSS colors: the claimed 'Forest Green' (#2E7D32) differs from standard #228B22, 'Amber' (#FF8F00) differs from standard #FFBF00, and 'Dark Blue' (#0D47A1) differs from standard #00008B.
